 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-33no","title":"Supply Chain Verification: Attestation + SBOM Gate","description":"# Supply Chain Verification: Attestation + SBOM Gate

## Goal
Block untrusted connector installs/updates by verifying signed supply-chain attestations and SBOMs before activation, with deterministic evidence bundles for audit and offline installs.

## Why (User Impact)
- Operators get cryptographic assurance that the binary matches audited provenance.
- Prevents downgrade/typosquat or mixed-identity artifacts from entering the mesh.
- Makes offline installs safer via cached, signed evidence.

## Scope
- Attestation + SBOM object schemas and signature verification.
- Host-side verification pipeline and policy gate during install/upgrade.
- CLI verify/report flows and machine-readable evidence bundles.
- Deterministic, redactable logs and receipts suitable for audits.

## Non-goals
- Implementing a full vulnerability scanner (we ingest/report external results).
- Replacing the registry; this is a gate on install/upgrade only.

## Deliverables
- Canonical schemas for SupplyChainAttestation and SBOM objects.
- Host verification pipeline (signature, provenance binding, policy checks).
- Evidence bundle object with stable hashes and redacted fields.
- CLI commands for verify/report/install gating.
- Unit/integration tests + E2E scripts with structured JSON logs.

## Security/Policy Notes
- Fail closed by default (missing/invalid attestation or SBOM blocks install).
- Support policy knobs: require_attestation, min_slsa_level, trusted_builders, allow_unsigned (dev-only).
- Verification must be deterministic (same inputs -> same evidence bundle hash).
- Never log secrets or raw tokens; redact sensitive fields in logs/evidence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Valid attestation+SBOM installs; invalid/unsigned is rejected unless explicit policy allows.
- Evidence bundle includes all verification steps, hashes, trust roots, and timestamps.
- Errors are actionable, deterministic, and redact secrets.
- Unit/integration tests cover positive/negative paths; E2E script passes in CI.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

Supply chain verification epic complete.","created_at":"2026-03-09T06:58:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-33qn","title":"[IDEA-08.3] Retry taxonomy tests + examples","description":"## Goal
Provide tests and examples for retry taxonomy helpers.

## Details
- Integration test with fake HTTP server returning retryable errors.
- Example showing how connectors should use retry policy in send/invoke paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests verify backoff and Retry-After behavior.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]

- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-33up","title":"UBS: remove panic! macros in health.rs tests","description":"UBS scan flagged critical panic! usage in fcp-core health tests. Replace panic branches with assert/matches to clear critical UBS findings, then rerun UBS.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.
[AlienArtifactReview] Updated 2026-02-02T23:58:59.240755+00:00","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlWolf","created_at":"2026-01-30T01:40:05.323665828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:57:31.080012065Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:57:31.079940612Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-343gd","title":"[FUZZ] fcp-raptorq::SymbolEnvelope::decrypt AEAD","description":"Fuzz the RaptorQ symbol AEAD decrypt path: fcp_raptorq::SymbolEnvelope::decrypt.

**Why not a trivial cargo-fuzz target:**
- decrypt() takes an envelope + AAD + sender subkey (32B). Random bytes from a libFuzzer input will virtually never produce an accepted AEAD tag, so a naive harness drifts into the InvalidTag rejection branch on 100% of inputs.
- To exercise the decrypt path for real, we need a structured harness that:
  1. Derives a valid sender subkey via derive_sender_subkey() with a fixed seed.
  2. Encrypts a victim plaintext via SymbolEnvelope::encrypt to produce a valid envelope + tag + nonce.
  3. Mutates one of {ciphertext, tag, aad, esi, frame_seq, sender_instance_id} by a fuzz-controlled bitflip.
  4. Calls decrypt() and asserts the AEAD authentication path correctly rejects every tampered variant (never returns Ok with mutated inputs).
- Plus a secondary corpus of hand-crafted envelopes: truncated ciphertext, oversized AAD, zero-length nonce-derivation inputs, ESI near u32::MAX, subkey material with known-weak patterns.

**Scope:**
- New target: fuzz/fuzz_targets/raptorq_envelope_decrypt.rs
- Seed corpus under fuzz/corpus/fuzz_raptorq_envelope_decrypt/ with at least 8 hand-crafted inputs
- Arbitrary-derived StructuredEnvelopeInput struct with encoded encrypt-then-tamper pipeline
- Assertion: every non-identity mutation yields DecryptFailed; identity mutation yields the original plaintext

**Context:** filed during FUZZING audit pane cod_4 / SunnyMoose (2026-04-23). Existing RaptorQ coverage is fuzz_raptorq_decode_bounds (symbol-flood admission control) and fuzz_mesh_post_verify_symbol_ack (mesh dispatch). Neither reaches the AEAD primitive in crates/fcp-raptorq/src/envelope.rs:127.

**DoD:**
- Target compiles + smoke-runs 60s at >1000 exec/s under ASan+UBSan.
- Corpus includes a positive round-trip seed and at least one tamper-of-each-field seed.
- No crashes; any found go into artifacts/ and file a new [CRITICAL] bead.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:08:00.202672Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07.529910Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-343gd","title":"[FUZZ] fcp-raptorq::SymbolEnvelope::decrypt AEAD","description":"Fuzz the RaptorQ symbol AEAD decrypt path: fcp_raptorq::SymbolEnvelope::decrypt.

**Why not a trivial cargo-fuzz target:**
- decrypt() takes an envelope + AAD + sender subkey (32B). Random bytes from a libFuzzer input will virtually never produce an accepted AEAD tag, so a naive harness drifts into the InvalidTag rejection branch on 100% of inputs.
- To exercise the decrypt path for real, we need a structured harness that:
  1. Derives a valid sender subkey via derive_sender_subkey() with a fixed seed.
  2. Encrypts a victim plaintext via SymbolEnvelope::encrypt to produce a valid envelope + tag + nonce.
  3. Mutates one of {ciphertext, tag, aad, esi, frame_seq, sender_instance_id} by a fuzz-controlled bitflip.
  4. Calls decrypt() and asserts the AEAD authentication path correctly rejects every tampered variant (never returns Ok with mutated inputs).
- Plus a secondary corpus of hand-crafted envelopes: truncated ciphertext, oversized AAD, zero-length nonce-derivation inputs, ESI near u32::MAX, subkey material with known-weak patterns.

**Scope:**
- New target: fuzz/fuzz_targets/raptorq_envelope_decrypt.rs
- Seed corpus under fuzz/corpus/fuzz_raptorq_envelope_decrypt/ with at least 8 hand-crafted inputs
- Arbitrary-derived StructuredEnvelopeInput struct with encoded encrypt-then-tamper pipeline
- Assertion: every non-identity mutation yields DecryptFailed; identity mutation yields the original plaintext

**Context:** filed during FUZZING audit pane cod_4 / SunnyMoose (2026-04-23). Existing RaptorQ coverage is fuzz_raptorq_decode_bounds (symbol-flood admission control) and fuzz_mesh_post_verify_symbol_ack (mesh dispatch). Neither reaches the AEAD primitive in crates/fcp-raptorq/src/envelope.rs:127.

**DoD:**
- Target compiles + smoke-runs 60s at >1000 exec/s under ASan+UBSan.
- Corpus includes a positive round-trip seed and at least one tamper-of-each-field seed.
- No crashes; any found go into artifacts/ and file a new [CRITICAL] bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:08:00.202672Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:50:33.942090Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:50:33.941736Z","close_reason":"Added structured SymbolEnvelope decrypt fuzz target with fixed-seed tamper corpus and smoke run","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-34lm","title":"CLI: fcp connector (list/info/introspect) - Agent-Visible Discovery","description":"# CLI: `fcp connector` (list/info/introspect) - Agent-Visible Discovery

## Goal
Implement connector discovery commands that agents (and operators) use to find and understand available connectors before invoking tools.

This fills the gap between Agent Integration (`1n78.30`) which defines the discovery API and the CLI which needs to expose it.

## Commands

### `fcp connector list`
List all registered connectors with summary info.

```bash
# Human output
fcp connector list
# ID              NAME          VERSION   TOOLS   RISK    STATUS
# fcp.discord     Discord       1.2.0     12      Risky   ✓ Healthy
# fcp.github      GitHub        2.0.1     24      Risky   ⚠ Degraded (rate limited)
# fcp.slack       Slack         1.0.0     8       Risky   ✓ Healthy
# fcp.anthropic   Anthropic     1.0.0     3       Safe    ✓ Healthy

# Filter by category
fcp connector list --category messaging
fcp connector list --category llm

# Filter by max risk tier
fcp connector list --max-risk safe
fcp connector list --max-risk moderate

# Filter by health
fcp connector list --healthy
fcp connector list --degraded

# JSON output for agents
fcp connector list --json
```

### `fcp connector info <connector>`
Show detailed connector information including manifest metadata.

```bash
fcp connector info fcp.discord
# Connector: fcp.discord
# Name: Discord
# Version: 1.2.0
# Archetype: Bidirectional
# Home Zone: z:private
# 
# Capabilities Required:
#   - discord.messages.read (Safe)
#   - discord.messages.send (Risky)
#   - discord.webhooks.manage (Dangerous)
# 
# Network Constraints:
#   Allowed: discord.com:443, gateway.discord.gg:443
#   CIDR Deny: default (RFC1918 + localhost)
# 
# State Model: singleton_writer
# Event Topics: message.new, message.edited, member.joined
# 
# Health: Healthy
# Uptime: 3d 14h 22m
# Last Health Check: 2s ago

fcp connector info fcp.discord --json
```

### `fcp connector introspect <connector>`
Return MCP-compatible tool descriptors for a connector.

```bash
# Human-readable tool list
fcp connector introspect fcp.discord
# Tools (12):
#   send_message      Send a message to a channel           Risky
#   send_dm           Send a direct message                 Risky  
#   list_channels     List channels in a guild              Safe
#   get_message       Get a specific message                Safe
#   ...

# Full MCP tool descriptors (JSON)
fcp connector introspect fcp.discord --json

# Single tool
fcp connector introspect fcp.discord --tool send_message --json
```

## Output Contracts

### ConnectorSummary (list)
```rust
pub struct ConnectorSummary {
    pub id: ConnectorId,
    pub name: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub version: semver::Version,
    pub categories: Vec<String>,
    pub tool_count: u32,
    pub max_risk_tier: SafetyTier,
    pub enabled: bool,
    pub health: ConnectorHealth,
}
```

### ConnectorInfo (info)
```rust
pub struct ConnectorInfo {
    pub summary: ConnectorSummary,
    pub archetype: ConnectorArchetype,
    pub home_zone: ZoneId,
    pub capabilities: CapabilityDeclarations,
    pub network_constraints: NetworkConstraints,
    pub state_model: ConnectorStateModel,
    pub event_topics: Vec<EventTopic>,
    pub manifest_hash: ObjectId,
    pub installed_at: DateTime<Utc>,
}
```

### ToolDescriptor (introspect) - SEP-1382 compliant
```rust
pub struct ToolDescriptor {
    pub name: String,
    pub description: String,
    pub input_schema: serde_json::Value,
    pub output_schema: serde_json::Value,
    pub risk_tier: SafetyTier,
    pub requires_confirmation: bool,
    pub idempotent: bool,
    pub supports_simulate: bool,
    pub latency_hint: LatencyHint,
    pub rate_limits: Option<Vec<String>>,
    pub examples: Option<Vec<ToolExample>>,
    pub ai_hints: Option<String>,
}
```

## Tests

### Unit Tests
- test_connector_list_filters_by_category
- test_connector_list_filters_by_risk
- test_connector_list_filters_by_health
- test_connector_info_includes_all_manifest_data
- test_connector_introspect_returns_mcp_compliant_descriptors
- test_connector_introspect_single_tool

### Integration Tests
- test_connector_list_matches_registry
- test_connector_introspect_schema_valid

### Snapshot Tests
- connector_list_human.snap
- connector_list_json.snap
- connector_info_human.snap
- connector_info_json.snap
- connector_introspect_human.snap
- connector_introspect_json.snap

### E2E Script
e2e/connector_discovery.sh with detailed logging

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `fcp connector list` returns all registered connectors
- [ ] Filtering by category, risk, and health works correctly
- [ ] `fcp connector info` returns full manifest-derived data
- [ ] `fcp connector introspect` returns MCP-compliant tool descriptors
- [ ] All commands support `--json` output
- [ ] No secrets are included in any output
- [ ] Commands work in offline mode with cached data","notes":"Claimed by OlivePuma to implement CLI list/info/introspect filters + align output with host discovery types.

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27","title":"[FCP3/P6.6] Capture a pre-cutover benchmark and transcript baseline for operator-critical flows","description":"Background: final cutover proof is much stronger if it compares against preserved pre deletion reality rather than against memory or improvised reruns after host first scaffolding is gone. Phase 6 is the first point where the CLI and operator surface are expected to be truthful on the new contracts, so it is the right moment to freeze a baseline before phase 7 deletions reshape the repo.

Scope: once CLI acceptance coverage and rewritten operator docs and transcripts are in place, capture a versioned pre cutover baseline bundle for the user visible and operator visible flows that later proof beads will judge. Include offline, node local, and mesh backed scenarios where available; command transcripts derived from scripted acceptance runs; structured logs; environment capture; latency and footprint snapshots; and notes about known transition seams so later comparisons can distinguish intentional architecture changes from regressions.

Acceptance: there is a preserved pre cutover artifact bundle that later phase 7 proof work can compare against, so the project can demonstrate not just that the final state works, but how it changed from the last truthful transitional state that users and operators would have seen.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:12:56.096644Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:11:36.043124Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:11:36.042829Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acceptance","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","performance","phase-6","proof","tests","transcripts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-24llg.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:34:20.945420Z","created_by":"Codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:13:03.413045Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-pl7pj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:13:06.249352Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2069,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27","author":"jemanuel","text":"Reason for this bead: without a preserved pre-deletion transcript and benchmark bundle, the final proof can only show that the end state works, not whether it preserved or improved the user-facing and operator-facing experience relative to the last truthful transitional state.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:14:16Z"},{"id":2602,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: All 3 children closed (34q27.1 scenario freeze, 34q27.2 transcript baseline, 34q27.3 performance baseline). Pre-cutover baseline is defined in docs/FCP3_Pre_Cutover_Baseline.md with 17 canonical scenarios, environment metadata, 5 known transition seams, and comparison protocol.","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:11:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27.1","title":"[FCP3/P6.6] Freeze the baseline scenario set and environment capture for pre-cutover proof","description":"Background: the pre-cutover baseline only matters if it preserves the right scenario set and the environment needed to interpret later comparisons.

Scope: freeze the baseline scenario set, environment capture, and comparison metadata for operator-critical flows. Define which commands and journeys count as canonical before phase-7 deletions begin, and record the environment, worker, topology, and known transition seams needed to interpret the captured artifacts honestly.

Acceptance: later performance and proof beads can compare against one preserved baseline definition instead of against fuzzy recollection or improvised reruns.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:55:00.954514Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:09:03.223177Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:09:03.222937Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","phase-6","proof","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:55:00.954514Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:52.269006Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2425,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27.1","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group baseline note: freeze these artifacts before phase-7 deletions erase the last truthful transitional state. Preserve environment capture, transcript and log schema versioning, benchmark diff protocol, and notes about intentional transition seams so later comparisons are reviewable and not anecdotal.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:17Z"},{"id":2597,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-34q27.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Created docs/FCP3_Pre_Cutover_Baseline.md defining: 17 canonical scenarios across 5 categories (discovery, lifecycle, invoke, truth resolution, security), environment capture metadata, 5 known transition seams, and comparison protocol for phase-7 proof. Every scenario cites its proof bead.","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:09:02Z"}]}
